versions.label: BSB
paragraphs: 11 When the people of Jabesh-gilead heard what the Philistines had done to Saul, 12 all their men of valor set out, journeyed all night, and retrieved the bodies of Saul and his sons from the wall of Beth-shan.
When they arrived at Jabesh, they burned the bodies there. 13 Then they took their bones and buried them under the tamarisk tree in Jabesh, and they fasted seven days.

context_lines: In **1 Samuel 31**, this section should be read within the flow of the chapter and the larger movement of 1 Samuel.
common_interpretation_lines: A common reading of **Jabesh-gilead’s Tribute to Saul** is that it should be interpreted in light of the chapter's full narrative movement rather than as an isolated proof text.
Where interpreters differ on details, the immediate setting in **1 Samuel 31** remains the first control on meaning.
